March-April 2006

An analysis of campus crime statistics highlights the University of Massachusetts as having the highest rate of violent crime, prompting discussions on safety measures.

January February 2006
Data & DatabasesPublic SafetyAgriculture & Land

After attending a CAR Boot Camp, the author utilizes CAR techniques to analyze farm accident data, showcasing the potential of data journalism in local reporting.

Public SafetyAgriculture & Land

An examination of rail-crossing safety following a fatal train accident, using data to reveal maintenance lapses.

MODELING REALITY Examining evacuation during nightclub fire

Paul Edward Parker July-August 2004
Public SafetyJournalism & NewsroomAgriculture & Land

Using computer simulations to analyze evacuation patterns during the Rhode Island nightclub fire, which killed 100 people, to identify potential improvements in safety regulations.

HIDDEN DANGERS Airlines and HazMat

Elizabeth Marchak December 1999
Public Safety

The article investigates ongoing issues with hazardous materials on airlines following the 1996 Valujet crash and evaluates the effectiveness of FAA's enforcement.

CAR HELPS UNCOVER THE DETAILS - Kennedy Crash

Andy Lehren October 1999
Public Safety

An in-depth analysis of John F. Kennedy Jr.'s plane crash using aviation databases to explore potential causes, highlighting spatial disorientation and pilot error.

Naming the unknown

Dawn Fallik June 1999
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsPublic Safety

Sampley's investigation led to the exhumation and identification of the Vietnam-era Unknown Soldier using military databases, revealing him to be Lt. Michael Joseph Blassie.

Stuart Watson May 1998

The report uncovers systemic issues in the military health care system that protect negligent practices, highlighting lack of malpractice insurance for military doctors.

Armstrong reveals safety failures in elevators and escalators through data analysis, leading to significant state-level repercussions.
